name: Speckled Alder
summary: The northern alder — better cold-and-elevation match for Pocono Plateau than smooth alder. Multi-stem thicket-forming shrub of cool streamsides, beaver-meadow edges, and saturated seeps. Nitrogen-fixing pioneer that builds soil on degraded ground.
notes: Alnus hosts 188+ Lepidoptera species including Baileya doubledayi (an alder specialist). Catkins provide critical early-spring pollen for emerging native bees. Nitrogen-fixing via Frankia bacteria symbiosis — improves soil chemistry over time. Reaches south into PA at higher elevations — Pocono Plateau is textbook habitat. Suckers aggressively (a feature for riparian restoration).

planting.aspect: Cool streamside; seeps; saturated wet hollows
planting.soil: Acidic to neutral; tolerates poor drainage and disturbed soils
planting.site_notes: Plant on cool wet sites at the 2,000 ft elevation — speckled alder outperforms smooth alder here. No deer protection needed. Excellent stabilizer for eroding stream banks. Pair with black willow for fast riparian cover.
